care_site_id.care_site_id: A unique identifier for each care site, serving as the primary key for the table.

care_site_name.care_site_name: The human‑readable name or title of the care site, describing its identity.

place_of_service_concept_id.place_of_service_concept_id: A foreign key referencing the concept table that defines the type of place of service for the care site.

location_id.location_id: A foreign key linking to the location table, indicating the physical location of the care site.

care_site_source_value.care_site_source_value: The original source value or code for the care site, used for data integration or provenance.

place_of_service_source_value.place_of_service_source_value: The original source value or code for the place of service concept, providing provenance for that attribute.

id.id: A unique identifier for each encounter record, serving as the primary key.

start.start: The timestamp when the encounter began, describing the start time of the visit.

stop.stop: The timestamp when the encounter ended, indicating the duration of the visit.

patient.patient: An identifier linking to the patient who experienced the encounter, acting as a foreign key.

provider.provider: An identifier linking to the healthcare provider who delivered care during the encounter, acting as a foreign key.

encounterclass.encounterclass: A categorical attribute describing the type of encounter (e.g., inpatient, outpatient, emergency).

code.code: A diagnostic or procedural code associated with the encounter, providing a standardized reference.

description.description: A textual description of the code, giving context to the diagnostic or procedural code.

cost.cost: A monetary measurement of the encounter’s cost, representing the financial value of the visit.

reasoncode.reasoncode: A code indicating the reason for the encounter, used for classification or billing purposes.

reasondescription.reasondescription: A textual explanation of the reasoncode, elaborating on why the encounter occurred.

person_id.person_id: A unique identifier for an individual, serving as the primary key linking death records to the person entity.

death_date.death_date: The calendar date on which the individual died, representing the temporal aspect of the death event.

death_datetime.death_datetime: The exact date and time of death, providing a more precise temporal measurement of the death event.

death_type_concept_id.death_type_concept_id: A foreign key referencing a concept that categorizes the type of death (e.g., natural, accidental), describing the nature of the death event.

cause_concept_id.cause_concept_id: A foreign key linking to a concept that identifies the underlying cause of death, providing a standardized classification of the cause.

cause_source_value.cause_source_value: The original source value or text description of the cause of death as recorded in the source system, offering unstandardized context.

cause_source_concept_id.cause_source_concept_id: A foreign key referencing the source concept identifier for the cause of death, linking the source value to a standardized concept.

id.id: A unique identifier for each care plan record, serving as the primary key for the careplans table.

start.start: The start date/time when the care plan becomes active, representing the temporal beginning of the plan.

stop.stop: The end date/time when the care plan is terminated or completed, representing the temporal conclusion of the plan.

patient.patient: A foreign key linking to the patient entity, indicating which patient the care plan applies to.

encounter.encounter: A foreign key linking to the encounter entity, indicating the clinical encounter during which the care plan was initiated.

code.code: An alphanumeric code that classifies the type or category of the care plan, serving as an attribute of the plan.

description.description: A textual description providing details about the care plan, elaborating on its purpose or contents.

reasoncode.reasoncode: A code representing the clinical reason or justification for the care plan, acting as an attribute of the plan.

reasondescription.reasondescription: A textual explanation of the reasoncode, giving context to the clinical justification for the care plan.

start.start: The date and time when the allergy was first noted for the patient, indicating the beginning of the allergy event.

stop.stop: The date and time when the allergy was considered resolved or no longer relevant for the patient, marking the end of the allergy event.

patient.patient: A unique identifier for the patient who has the allergy, serving as the primary key linking the record to the patient entity.

encounter.encounter: A foreign key referencing the specific clinical encounter during which the allergy was documented or assessed, linking the allergy to a particular visit.

code.code: The standardized code (e.g., ICD-10 or SNOMED) representing the specific allergy, providing a categorical classification of the allergic reaction.

description.description: A free-text description of the allergy, offering additional details or context about the allergic reaction.

cohort_definition_id.cohort_definition_id: An identifier for the specific cohort definition that the subject belongs to, serving as a foreign key linking to the cohort definition entity.

subject_id.subject_id: A unique identifier for the individual subject, acting as the primary key for the cohort membership record.

cohort_start_date.cohort_start_date: The date when the subject entered the cohort, indicating the start of the cohort membership period.

cohort_end_date.cohort_end_date: The date when the subject exited the cohort, indicating the end of the cohort membership period.

location_id.location_id: A unique identifier for each location, serving as the primary key for the location entity.

address_1.address_1: The primary street address line for the location, describing its physical address.

address_2.address_2: An optional secondary address line (e.g., suite or apartment number) for the location.

city.city: The city in which the location is situated.

state.state: The state or province where the location resides.

zip.zip: The postal code associated with the location's address.

county.county: The county or administrative region containing the location.

location_source_value.location_source_value: A value indicating the source or origin of the location data, possibly referencing an external system.

cdm_source_name.cdm_source_name: The name of the Common Data Model source, serving as the primary identifier for this source entity.

cdm_source_abbreviation.cdm_source_abbreviation: An abbreviated form of the source name, providing a concise identifier for the source.

cdm_holder.cdm_holder: The organization or entity that holds or maintains the Common Data Model source.

source_description.source_description: A textual description of the source, detailing its purpose and contents.

source_documentation_reference.source_documentation_reference: A reference to documentation that provides additional information about the source.

cdm_etl_reference.cdm_etl_reference: A reference to the ETL process used to load data into the Common Data Model source.

source_release_date.source_release_date: The date when the source was released, indicating the version's availability date.

cdm_release_date.cdm_release_date: The date when the Common Data Model version was released, marking the version's publication date.

cdm_version.cdm_version: The version number of the Common Data Model used by this source.

vocabulary_version.vocabulary_version: The version number of the vocabulary associated with this source.

date.date: The date on which the immunization was administered, indicating when the event occurred for the encounter.

patient.patient: An identifier linking to the patient who received the immunization, representing the individual associated with the encounter.

encounter.encounter: A unique identifier for the clinical encounter during which the immunization was given, serving as the primary key for this record.

code.code: The standardized code representing the specific immunization administered, identifying the vaccine type for the encounter.

description.description: A textual description of the immunization code, providing details about the vaccine administered during the encounter.

cost.cost: The monetary cost associated with administering the immunization, indicating the financial expense for the encounter.

device_exposure_id.device_exposure_id: A unique identifier for each device exposure record, serving as the primary key for the table.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking the device exposure to a specific person, indicating who used or was exposed to the device.

device_concept_id.device_concept_id: A concept identifier that specifies the type of device used, referencing the device vocabulary.

device_exposure_start_date.device_exposure_start_date: The calendar date when the device exposure began, providing the start point of the exposure period.

device_exposure_start_datetime.device_exposure_start_datetime: The exact timestamp (date and time) when the device exposure started, offering precise temporal resolution.

device_exposure_end_date.device_exposure_end_date: The calendar date when the device exposure ended, marking the conclusion of the exposure period.

device_exposure_end_datetime.device_exposure_end_datetime: The exact timestamp (date and time) when the device exposure ended, providing precise end time.

device_type_concept_id.device_type_concept_id: A concept identifier indicating the category or type of device (e.g., medical device, consumer device), linking to device type vocabulary.

unique_device_id.unique_device_id: A unique identifier for the specific device instance, such as a serial number, distinguishing it from other devices.

quantity.quantity: The number of device units involved in the exposure, representing the quantity used or observed.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking the exposure to the healthcare provider who administered or recorded the device, indicating the responsible provider.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: A foreign key referencing the visit during which the device exposure occurred, tying the exposure to a specific patient visit.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: A foreign key referencing a detailed visit event, providing more granular context for the exposure within a visit.

device_source_value.device_source_value: The original source value or code for the device as recorded in the source system, useful for traceability.

device_source_concept_id.device_source_concept_id: A concept identifier mapping the source device code to the standardized vocabulary, facilitating integration.

condition_occurrence_id.condition_occurrence_id: A unique identifier for each condition occurrence record, serving as the primary key for tracking individual condition events.

person_id.person_id: A foreign key linking to the person entity, identifying the individual who experienced the condition.

condition_concept_id.condition_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table that specifies the standardized concept code for the condition.

condition_start_date.condition_start_date: The calendar date when the condition began, providing a temporal attribute of the condition occurrence.

condition_start_datetime.condition_start_datetime: The precise date and time when the condition started, offering more detailed temporal information.

condition_end_date.condition_end_date: The calendar date when the condition ended, indicating the duration of the condition occurrence.

condition_end_datetime.condition_end_datetime: The exact date and time when the condition concluded, giving precise temporal resolution.

condition_type_concept_id.condition_type_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table that defines the type of condition occurrence (e.g., diagnosis, problem list).

condition_status_concept_id.condition_status_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table that indicates the current status of the condition (e.g., active, resolved).

stop_reason.stop_reason: A free‑text field describing the reason the condition was terminated or resolved.

provider_id.provider_id: A foreign key to the provider entity, identifying the healthcare professional who recorded or treated the condition.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: A foreign key linking the condition to a specific visit occurrence, associating it with a broader encounter.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: A foreign key to a more granular visit detail entity, providing detailed context for the visit during which the condition was recorded.

condition_source_value.condition_source_value: The original source value for the condition concept, capturing the raw terminology used in the source data.

condition_source_concept_id.condition_source_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table that represents the source concept identifier for the condition.

condition_status_source_value.condition_status_source_value: The original source text for the condition status, preserving the terminology used in the source data.

cohort_definition_id.cohort_definition_id: A unique identifier for each cohort definition, serving as the primary key for the cohort_definition table.

cohort_definition_name.cohort_definition_name: The human-readable name of the cohort definition, describing its purpose or characteristics.

cohort_definition_description.cohort_definition_description: A textual description providing additional details about the cohort definition, elaborating on its intent or scope.

definition_type_concept_id.definition_type_concept_id: A foreign key referencing a concept that categorizes the type of definition (e.g., SQL, CDM), linking the cohort to its definition methodology.

cohort_definition_syntax.cohort_definition_syntax: The syntax or query string that specifies the logic for selecting individuals into the cohort, defining the cohort’s composition.

subject_concept_id.subject_concept_id: A foreign key referencing the concept that identifies the subject of the cohort (e.g., patient, visit), linking the cohort to the entity it describes.

cohort_initiation_date.cohort_initiation_date: The date when the cohort definition was created or activated, indicating the temporal context of the definition.

id.id: A unique identifier for each imaging study, serving as the primary key for the record.

date.date: The date on which the imaging study was performed, indicating the timing of the procedure.

patient.patient: An identifier linking to the patient who underwent the imaging study, representing the subject of the examination.

encounter.encounter: An identifier linking to the clinical encounter during which the imaging study was ordered or performed, indicating the context of care.

body site code.body site code: A standardized code representing the anatomical location examined in the imaging study, used for classification and interoperability.

body site description.body site description: A textual description of the anatomical site corresponding to the body site code, providing human-readable context.

modality code.modality code: A standardized code indicating the imaging modality (e.g., CT, MRI, X-ray) used for the study, facilitating categorization.

modality description.modality description: A textual description of the imaging modality corresponding to the modality code, clarifying the type of imaging performed.

sop code.sop code: A code identifying the specific Service Object Pair (SOP) class used in the DICOM image, indicating the technical format of the study.

sop description.sop description: A textual description of the SOP class corresponding to the sop code, explaining the image type and structure.

domain_concept_id_1.domain_concept_id_1: The domain concept identifier for the first fact in the relationship, indicating the type of fact entity involved. It is an attribute of the relationship defined by relationship_concept_id.

fact_id_1.fact_id_1: The unique identifier of the first fact record participating in the relationship. It is an attribute of the relationship defined by relationship_concept_id.

domain_concept_id_2.domain_concept_id_2: The domain concept identifier for the second fact in the relationship, specifying the type of the second fact entity. It is an attribute of the relationship defined by relationship_concept_id.

fact_id_2.fact_id_2: The unique identifier of the second fact record involved in the relationship. It is an attribute of the relationship defined by relationship_concept_id.

relationship_concept_id.relationship_concept_id: The concept identifier that defines the nature of the relationship between the two fact records, serving as the primary descriptor of the relationship entity.

start.start: The timestamp indicating when the condition began for the patient, marking the start of the clinical event.

stop.stop: The timestamp indicating when the condition ended for the patient, marking the end of the clinical event.

patient.patient: A unique identifier for the patient, serving as the primary key linking all conditions to the individual.

encounter.encounter: A foreign key referencing the specific encounter during which the condition was recorded, linking the condition to a particular visit.

code.code: An identifier for the medical condition (e.g., ICD code), describing the diagnosis associated with the patient.

description.description: A textual description of the condition, providing a human-readable explanation of the diagnosis.

id.id: A unique identifier for each organization, serving as the primary key for the table.

name.name: The official name of the organization, describing its identity.

address.address: The street address of the organization’s primary location.

city.city: The city where the organization is located.

state.state: The state or province of the organization’s location.

zip.zip: The postal code for the organization’s address.

phone.phone: The primary telephone number for contacting the organization.

utilization.utilization: A measurement of the organization’s resource usage or capacity utilization, indicating how much of its available resources are in use.

observation_period_id.observation_period_id: A unique identifier for each observation period, serving as the primary key for the table.

person_id.person_id: A foreign key linking to the person entity, identifying the individual associated with this observation period.

observation_period_start_date.observation_period_start_date: The start date of the observation period, indicating when the period begins.

observation_period_end_date.observation_period_end_date: The end date of the observation period, indicating when the period concludes.

period_type_concept_id.period_type_concept_id: A concept identifier that classifies the type of observation period, referencing a concept table for categorical context.

measurement_id.measurement_id: A unique identifier for each measurement record, serving as the primary key for the measurement entity.

person_id.person_id: A foreign key linking to the person entity, identifying the patient on whom the measurement was taken.

measurement_concept_id.measurement_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table that specifies the type of measurement (e.g., blood pressure, glucose level).

measurement_date.measurement_date: The calendar date when the measurement was recorded, providing a temporal reference for the measurement.

measurement_datetime.measurement_datetime: The full timestamp (date and time) of when the measurement was taken, offering precise temporal context.

measurement_time.measurement_time: The time component of the measurement, indicating the time of day the measurement was performed.

measurement_type_concept_id.measurement_type_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table indicating the classification of the measurement (e.g., observation, vital sign).

operator_concept_id.operator_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table that identifies the method or operator used to obtain the measurement.

value_as_number.value_as_number: The numeric value of the measurement, representing the quantitative result.

value_as_concept_id.value_as_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table indicating a coded categorical value when the measurement is not numeric.

unit_concept_id.unit_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table specifying the unit of measurement (e.g., mmHg, mg/dL).

range_low.range_low: The lower bound of the normal reference range for this measurement, providing clinical context.

range_high.range_high: The upper bound of the normal reference range for this measurement, providing clinical context.

provider_id.provider_id: A foreign key to the provider entity indicating the clinician or staff who performed the measurement.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: A foreign key to the visit entity linking the measurement to the specific healthcare visit during which it was taken.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: A foreign key to the visit detail entity offering more granular visit context for the measurement.

measurement_source_value.measurement_source_value: The original source string value for the measurement, capturing the raw data as entered.

measurement_source_concept_id.measurement_source_concept_id: A foreign key to the concept table representing the source concept for the measurement.

unit_source_value.unit_source_value: The original source string for the unit of measurement, preserving the raw unit data.

value_source_value.value_source_value: The original source string for the measurement value, preserving the raw value data.

payer_plan_period_id.payer_plan_period_id: A unique identifier for each payer plan period, serving as the primary key for the coverage record.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking the period to a specific individual, representing the person covered during this period.

payer_plan_period_start_date.payer_plan_period_start_date: The date when the payer plan coverage begins for the person, indicating the start of the period.

payer_plan_period_end_date.payer_plan_period_end_date: The date when the payer plan coverage ends for the person, indicating the end of the period.

payer_source_value.payer_source_value: A categorical value describing the payer (e.g., insurance company) associated with this coverage period.

plan_source_value.plan_source_value: A categorical value indicating the specific plan type or identifier linked to this coverage period.

family_source_value.family_source_value: A categorical value representing the family context or group associated with this coverage period.

drug_exposure_id.drug_exposure_id: A unique identifier for each drug exposure record, serving as the primary key for the table.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking the drug exposure to the patient, referencing the person entity.

drug_concept_id.drug_concept_id: A foreign key to the standardized drug concept table, indicating the specific medication prescribed.

drug_exposure_start_date.drug_exposure_start_date: The calendar date when the drug exposure began, representing the start of the prescription period.

drug_exposure_start_datetime.drug_exposure_start_datetime: The exact date and time when the drug exposure started, providing precise timing of the prescription initiation.

drug_exposure_end_date.drug_exposure_end_date: The calendar date when the drug exposure ended, marking the conclusion of the prescription period.

drug_exposure_end_datetime.drug_exposure_end_datetime: The exact date and time when the drug exposure ended, giving precise timing of the prescription termination.

verbatim_end_date.verbatim_end_date: The original end date recorded in the source data, preserved for audit and comparison purposes.

drug_type_concept_id.drug_type_concept_id: A foreign key to the drug type concept table, categorizing the nature of the drug exposure (e.g., prescription, over‑the‑counter).

stop_reason.stop_reason: A categorical description of why the drug exposure was discontinued (e.g., completed, adverse reaction).

refills.refills: The number of refills authorized for the prescription, indicating repeat dispensing.

quantity.quantity: The total quantity of the drug prescribed, measured in the drug's unit of measure.

days_supply.days_supply: The number of days the prescribed quantity is intended to cover, reflecting dosage frequency.

sig.sig: Textual instructions for how the patient should take the medication, such as dosage and frequency.

route_concept_id.route_concept_id: A foreign key to the route of administration concept table, specifying how the drug is to be taken (e.g., oral, intravenous).

lot_number.lot_number: The batch or lot identifier for the drug product, used for traceability.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier for the healthcare provider who prescribed or dispensed the medication, linking to the provider entity.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: A foreign key to the visit occurrence table, indicating the clinical encounter during which the drug exposure was recorded.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: A foreign key to the visit detail table, providing more granular visit information related to the drug exposure.

drug_source_value.drug_source_value: The original source value for the drug, as recorded in the data source, before mapping to standardized concepts.

drug_source_concept_id.drug_source_concept_id: The source concept identifier for the drug, used when the source concept does not map to a standardized concept.

route_source_value.route_source_value: The original source value for the route of administration, before mapping to standardized concepts.

dose_unit_source_value.dose_unit_source_value: The original source value for the dose unit, preserving the unit used in the source data.

date.date: The date when the observation was recorded, indicating the timing of the measurement for the patient.

patient.patient: A unique identifier for the patient, serving as the primary key linking observations to the individual.

encounter.encounter: An identifier linking to the specific clinical encounter during which the observation was taken, representing the context of the measurement.

code.code: A standardized code (e.g., LOINC) identifying the type of observation, used to classify the measurement.

description.description: A textual description of the observation, providing human-readable details about the measurement.

value.value: The numeric or qualitative value of the observation, representing the measured quantity.

units.units: The units of measurement for the value, indicating the scale or system used.

type.type: A categorical label classifying the observation (e.g., vital sign, lab test), describing its nature.

observation_id.observation_id: A unique identifier for each observation record, serving as the primary key.

person_id.person_id: A foreign key linking to the person who had the observation, identifying the individual.

observation_concept_id.observation_concept_id: A concept ID referencing the standardized vocabulary for the observation type (e.g., measurement, symptom).

observation_date.observation_date: The calendar date when the observation was recorded, a temporal attribute.

observation_datetime.observation_datetime: The full timestamp of the observation, providing precise timing.

observation_type_concept_id.observation_type_concept_id: A concept ID indicating the type of observation event (e.g., vital sign, lab result).

value_as_number.value_as_number: The numeric value of the observation, representing a quantitative measurement.

value_as_string.value_as_string: A string representation of the observation value, used when a numeric value is not applicable.

value_as_concept_id.value_as_concept_id: A concept ID for the observation value when it is a coded concept.

qualifier_concept_id.qualifier_concept_id: A concept ID for a qualifier of the observation (e.g., side of body, method).

unit_concept_id.unit_concept_id: A concept ID for the unit of measurement associated with the observation value.

provider_id.provider_id: A foreign key to the provider who recorded the observation, indicating the clinician.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: A foreign key to the visit during which the observation was made.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: A foreign key to a detailed visit record, providing finer visit context.

observation_source_value.observation_source_value: The original source string value for the observation, as entered in the source system.

observation_source_concept_id.observation_source_concept_id: The concept ID derived from the source value, mapping it to a standardized concept.

unit_source_value.unit_source_value: The original source string for the unit of measurement.

qualifier_source_value.qualifier_source_value: The original source string for the qualifier of the observation.

observation_event_id.observation_event_id: A foreign key to the observation event record that groups related observation fields.

obs_event_field_concept_id.obs_event_field_concept_id: A concept ID describing the specific field within the observation event.

value_as_datetime.value_as_datetime: A datetime value of the observation when the measurement itself is a date/time.

id.id: A unique identifier for each provider, serving as the primary key for the providers table.

organization.organization: The name of the organization or practice with which the provider is affiliated, describing the provider's employer.

name.name: The full name of the provider, identifying the individual.

gender.gender: The provider's gender, a categorical attribute indicating sex.

speciality.speciality: The medical specialty or area of expertise of the provider, classifying the type of care they deliver.

address.address: The street address of the provider's office or practice location.

city.city: The city component of the provider's office address.

state.state: The state or province component of the provider's office address.

zip.zip: The postal code for the provider's office address.

utilization.utilization: A quantitative measure of the provider's utilization, such as the number of patients seen or hours worked, indicating workload.

start.start: The start date/time when the medication was initiated for the patient, indicating the beginning of the medication event.

stop.stop: The stop date/time when the medication was discontinued for the patient, indicating the end of the medication event.

patient.patient: An identifier for the patient receiving the medication, serving as the primary key for the patient entity.

encounter.encounter: An identifier linking to the specific encounter during which the medication was administered, representing the clinical context.

code.code: A unique code identifying the medication prescribed or administered, referencing the medication catalog.

description.description: A textual description of the medication, providing details such as drug name and formulation.

cost.cost: The monetary cost associated with the medication for this administration, representing the financial value.

reasoncode.reasoncode: A code indicating the clinical reason for prescribing the medication, referencing a standardized reason taxonomy.

reasondescription.reasondescription: A textual description of the reason for the medication, elaborating on the clinical indication.

date.date: The date on which the procedure was performed, indicating the timing of the event within the encounter.

patient.patient: An identifier linking to the patient who underwent the procedure, representing the patient associated with the encounter.

encounter.encounter: A unique identifier for each patient visit or encounter, serving as the primary key for the procedure record.

code.code: A standardized procedure code (e.g., CPT) that categorizes the type of medical procedure performed.

description.description: A textual description of the procedure, providing details about the performed service.

cost.cost: The monetary amount charged for the procedure, representing its financial value.

reasoncode.reasoncode: A code indicating the clinical reason or indication for performing the procedure.

reasondescription.reasondescription: A textual explanation of the reasoncode, describing why the procedure was necessary.

person_id.person_id: A unique identifier for each person, serving as the primary key for the person entity.

gender_concept_id.gender_concept_id: A foreign key linking to the concept table that defines the person's gender, describing the gender attribute of the person.

year_of_birth.year_of_birth: The year component of the person's birth date, providing a temporal attribute of the person's age.

month_of_birth.month_of_birth: The month component of the person's birth date, providing a temporal attribute of the person's age.

day_of_birth.day_of_birth: The day component of the person's birth date, providing a temporal attribute of the person's age.

birth_datetime.birth_datetime: The full timestamp of the person's birth, offering a precise temporal record of birth.

death_datetime.death_datetime: The timestamp of the person's death, if applicable, indicating the end of the person's life.

race_concept_id.race_concept_id: A foreign key linking to the concept table that defines the person's race, describing the race attribute of the person.

ethnicity_concept_id.ethnicity_concept_id: A foreign key linking to the concept table that defines the person's ethnicity, describing the ethnicity attribute of the person.

location_id.location_id: A foreign key linking to the location table that specifies the person's residence or associated location.

provider_id.provider_id: A foreign key linking to the provider table that identifies the healthcare provider associated with the person.

care_site_id.care_site_id: A foreign key linking to the care site table that indicates the care site related to the person.

person_source_value.person_source_value: The original source identifier for the person, preserving the external reference used in the source system.

gender_source_value.gender_source_value: The original source value for the person's gender, preserving the external representation of gender.

gender_source_concept_id.gender_source_concept_id: A foreign key linking to the concept table that defines the source concept for gender, providing context for the source gender value.

race_source_value.race_source_value: The original source value for the person's race, preserving the external representation of race.

race_source_concept_id.race_source_concept_id: A foreign key linking to the concept table that defines the source concept for race, providing context for the source race value.

ethnicity_source_value.ethnicity_source_value: The original source value for the person's ethnicity, preserving the external representation of ethnicity.

ethnicity_source_concept_id.ethnicity_source_concept_id: A foreign key linking to the concept table that defines the source concept for ethnicity, providing context for the source ethnicity value.

id.id: A unique identifier for each patient, serving as the primary key for patient records.

birthdate.birthdate: The patient's date of birth, an attribute indicating their age and birth date.

deathdate.deathdate: The patient's date of death, if applicable, marking the end of their life.

ssn.ssn: The patient's Social Security Number, an additional personal identifier used for national identification.

drivers.drivers: The patient's driver's license number, serving as another personal identifier.

passport.passport: The patient's passport number, used for international identification.

prefix.prefix: The title or honorific preceding the patient's name (e.g., Mr., Ms., Dr.), an attribute of the patient.

first.first: The patient's given (first) name, an attribute of the patient.

last.last: The patient's family (last) name, an attribute of the patient.

suffix.suffix: The suffix following the patient's name (e.g., Jr., III), an attribute of the patient.

maiden.maiden: The patient's maiden name, if applicable, an attribute of the patient.

marital.marital: The patient's marital status (e.g., single, married), an attribute of the patient.

race.race: The patient's self-identified race, an attribute of the patient.

ethnicity.ethnicity: The patient's self-identified ethnicity, an attribute of the patient.

gender.gender: The patient's gender identity, an attribute of the patient.

birthplace.birthplace: The location where the patient was born, an attribute of the patient.

address.address: The patient's street address, an attribute of the patient.

city.city: The city component of the patient's address, an attribute of the patient.

state.state: The state or province component of the patient's address, an attribute of the patient.

zip.zip: The postal code of the patient's address, an attribute of the patient.

note_id.note_id: A unique identifier for each note, serving as the primary key for the note entity.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the person who authored or is associated with the note, establishing a foreign key relationship.

note_event_id.note_event_id: An identifier referencing the event that the note describes, acting as a foreign key to the event entity.

note_event_field_concept_id.note_event_field_concept_id: A concept ID that specifies the particular field of the event the note pertains to, providing contextual detail.

note_date.note_date: The calendar date when the note was created, indicating the timing of the note.

note_datetime.note_datetime: The exact date and time when the note was created, offering precise temporal information.

note_type_concept_id.note_type_concept_id: A concept ID that categorizes the type of note (e.g., clinical, administrative), describing its classification.

note_class_concept_id.note_class_concept_id: A concept ID that classifies the note into a broader class (e.g., progress, discharge summary), adding categorical context.

note_title.note_title: The title or headline of the note, summarizing its content.

note_text.note_text: The full textual content of the note, containing detailed information.

encoding_concept_id.encoding_concept_id: A concept ID indicating the encoding scheme used for the note text, specifying how the text is represented.

language_concept_id.language_concept_id: A concept ID indicating the language in which the note is written, providing linguistic context.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the provider who authored the note, establishing a foreign key relationship.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: An identifier linking to the visit during which the note was made, connecting the note to a specific encounter.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: An identifier linking to a detailed visit record, offering more granular encounter information.

note_source_value.note_source_value: The original source value of the note, preserving the raw identifier from the source system.

provider_id.provider_id: A unique identifier for each provider, serving as the primary key for the provider entity.

provider_name.provider_name: The name of the provider, describing the provider’s identity.

npi.npi: The National Provider Identifier, a unique number assigned to the provider for billing and identification purposes.

dea.dea: The Drug Enforcement Administration number assigned to the provider, used for prescribing controlled substances.

specialty_concept_id.specialty_concept_id: A foreign key referencing the concept table that defines the provider’s specialty.

care_site_id.care_site_id: A foreign key linking to the care site where the provider practices.

year_of_birth.year_of_birth: The provider’s year of birth, indicating the provider’s age.

gender_concept_id.gender_concept_id: A foreign key referencing the concept table that defines the provider’s gender.

provider_source_value.provider_source_value: The original source value for the provider identifier from the data source.

specialty_source_value.specialty_source_value: The original source value for the provider’s specialty from the data source.

specialty_source_concept_id.specialty_source_concept_id: The source concept ID for the provider’s specialty, linking to the source concept table.

gender_source_value.gender_source_value: The original source value for the provider’s gender from the data source.

gender_source_concept_id.gender_source_concept_id: The source concept ID for the provider’s gender, linking to the source concept table.

procedure_occurrence_id.procedure_occurrence_id: A unique identifier for each procedure event, serving as the primary key for the procedure_occurrence table.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the person (patient) who underwent the procedure, establishing the patient context for this event.

procedure_concept_id.procedure_concept_id: A foreign key referencing the standardized concept that defines the type of procedure performed.

procedure_date.procedure_date: The calendar date on which the procedure was performed, providing a temporal reference for the event.

procedure_datetime.procedure_datetime: The exact date and time of the procedure, offering precise temporal detail for the event.

procedure_type_concept_id.procedure_type_concept_id: A foreign key to a concept that classifies the procedure’s type (e.g., diagnostic, therapeutic) within the event.

modifier_concept_id.modifier_concept_id: A foreign key to a concept providing additional modifier information for the procedure, refining its description.

quantity.quantity: A measurement indicating how many times the procedure was performed during this event.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier linking to the provider who performed the procedure, identifying the clinician responsible.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: A foreign key to the visit during which the procedure occurred, connecting the event to the broader visit context.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: A foreign key to a more detailed visit record, offering granular visit-level information for the procedure.

procedure_source_value.procedure_source_value: The original source system code for the procedure, preserving the raw coding used in the source data.

procedure_source_concept_id.procedure_source_concept_id: A concept id that maps the source code to the standardized concept, linking source and standardized vocabularies.

modifier_source_value.modifier_source_value: The original source system code for the modifier, maintaining the raw modifier code from the source data.

specimen_id.specimen_id: A unique identifier for each specimen record, serving as the primary key for the specimen entity.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking the specimen to the individual from whom it was collected, representing the subject of the specimen.

specimen_concept_id.specimen_concept_id: A concept identifier that specifies the type of specimen collected, describing the specimen's nature.

specimen_type_concept_id.specimen_type_concept_id: A concept identifier indicating the classification of the specimen type, providing categorical information about the specimen.

specimen_date.specimen_date: The calendar date when the specimen was collected, indicating the timing of the collection event.

specimen_datetime.specimen_datetime: The precise date and time of specimen collection, offering a more granular temporal reference.

quantity.quantity: The measured amount of specimen collected, representing a quantitative value of the specimen.

unit_concept_id.unit_concept_id: A concept identifier for the unit of measurement used for the quantity, describing how the quantity is expressed.

anatomic_site_concept_id.anatomic_site_concept_id: A concept identifier indicating the anatomical location from which the specimen was obtained, providing contextual information about the specimen source.

disease_status_concept_id.disease_status_concept_id: A concept identifier representing the disease status of the specimen source at the time of collection, categorizing the specimen's health context.

specimen_source_id.specimen_source_id: An identifier from the source system that uniquely references this specimen record, linking to external data.

specimen_source_value.specimen_source_value: A source system value that provides a human-readable identifier for the specimen, often used for cross-referencing.

unit_source_value.unit_source_value: The source system's representation of the unit of measurement for the specimen quantity.

anatomic_site_source_value.anatomic_site_source_value: The source system's textual description of the anatomical site from which the specimen was taken.

disease_status_source_value.disease_status_source_value: The source system's textual description of the disease status associated with the specimen.

visit_detail_id.visit_detail_id: A unique identifier for each visit detail record, serving as the primary key for the visit detail entity.

person_id.person_id: A foreign key linking to the person who experienced the visit, identifying the individual associated with this visit detail.

visit_detail_concept_id.visit_detail_concept_id: A categorical code that defines the type of visit detail, referencing the concept table for classification.

visit_detail_start_date.visit_detail_start_date: The calendar date when the visit detail began, indicating the start of the event.

visit_detail_start_datetime.visit_detail_start_datetime: The full date and time when the visit detail started, providing precise temporal information.

visit_detail_end_date.visit_detail_end_date: The calendar date when the visit detail ended, marking the conclusion of the event.

visit_detail_end_datetime.visit_detail_end_datetime: The full date and time when the visit detail ended, offering exact temporal resolution.

visit_detail_type_concept_id.visit_detail_type_concept_id: A concept identifier indicating the type of visit detail, used for categorization.

provider_id.provider_id: A foreign key to the provider entity, identifying the healthcare professional involved in the visit detail.

care_site_id.care_site_id: A foreign key to the care site entity, specifying the location where the visit detail occurred.

visit_detail_source_value.visit_detail_source_value: The original source system value for the visit detail, used for traceability.

visit_detail_source_concept_id.visit_detail_source_concept_id: A concept identifier for the source value, linking it to a standardized concept.

admitted_from_source_value.admitted_from_source_value: The source system value indicating where the patient was admitted from.

admitted_from_concept_id.admitted_from_concept_id: A concept identifier for the admission source, providing standardized classification.

discharge_to_source_value.discharge_to_source_value: The source system value indicating where the patient was discharged to.

discharge_to_concept_id.discharge_to_concept_id: A concept identifier for the discharge destination, offering standardized classification.

preceding_visit_detail_id.preceding_visit_detail_id: A foreign key referencing the previous visit detail in a sequence, indicating order.

visit_detail_parent_id.visit_detail_parent_id: A foreign key to a parent visit detail, establishing a hierarchical relationship.

visit_occurrence_id.visit_occurrence_id: A foreign key linking to the overall visit occurrence record, connecting this detail to the broader visit event.
A unique identifier for each visit record, serving as the primary key for tracking individual visits.

person_id.person_id: An identifier linking to the patient who experienced the visit, representing the person associated with this record.

visit_concept_id.visit_concept_id: A standardized concept ID that describes the type of visit (e.g., inpatient, outpatient), providing a coded attribute of the visit.

visit_start_date.visit_start_date: The calendar date when the visit began, indicating the start of the encounter.

visit_start_datetime.visit_start_datetime: The precise date and time when the visit started, offering a more detailed temporal attribute.

visit_end_date.visit_end_date: The calendar date when the visit concluded, marking the end of the encounter.

visit_end_datetime.visit_end_datetime: The exact date and time when the visit ended, giving a detailed temporal endpoint.

visit_type_concept_id.visit_type_concept_id: A concept ID that categorizes the visit type (e.g., emergency, scheduled), serving as a categorical attribute.

provider_id.provider_id: An identifier for the healthcare provider who delivered care during the visit, linking to the provider entity.

care_site_id.care_site_id: An identifier for the care site (hospital, clinic) where the visit took place, linking to the care site entity.

visit_source_value.visit_source_value: The original source code or value for the visit type from the data source, capturing source-specific information.

visit_source_concept_id.visit_source_concept_id: The standardized concept ID that maps the source value to a common vocabulary, aligning source data with a controlled terminology.

admitted_from_concept_id.admitted_from_concept_id: A concept ID indicating the source of admission (e.g., ER, transfer) from a standardized vocabulary, providing categorical context.

admitted_from_source_value.admitted_from_source_value: The original source value for the admission source, preserving the source system’s terminology.

discharge_to_concept_id.discharge_to_concept_id: A concept ID indicating the destination after discharge (e.g., home, rehab) from a standardized vocabulary, offering categorical detail.

discharge_to_source_value.discharge_to_source_value: The original source value for the discharge destination, maintaining source-specific terminology.

preceding_visit_occurrence_id.preceding_visit_occurrence_id: An identifier referencing a prior visit that preceded this one, establishing a sequence relationship between visits.
